item.name: 🔐 Quick Auth Setup → Then Run APIs

description.content:
This folder contains a curated set of requests for rapid Docusign integration. By configuring authentication at the folder level, you can execute all contained requests without manual per request setup. Successful execution of the authentication flows within this folder will populate the **Environment and Collection variables** ``{{​access_token}}`` and ``{{​refresh_token}}``. Once these values are saved to your active environment, they will be globally available for all requests in this folder. Every request in this folder is configured with auth type as **inherit auth from the parent**. To ensure seamless execution, the folder itself is set to the **Bearer Token** authorization type, using ``{{​access_token}}`` as the value. **Note**: When viewing the Folder's **Authorization** tab, ensure the **Token** field contains only the variable ``{{​access_token}}``. Do not include the word "Bearer" in the text box, as Postman automatically generates the correct ``Authorization: Bearer [token]`` header. ## **Choose your Authentication Type** Docusign uses OAuth 2.0 to secure your API requests. Using OAuth, your app requests access and performs actions, such as requesting a signature, on behalf of one of your users. Docusign provides four scenarios for implementing OAuth: - [Authenticate with the JWT Grant](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/auth/jwt-get-token/) - [Authenticate with the Confidential Authorization Code Grant](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/auth/confidential-authcode-get-token/) - [Authenticate with Public Authorization Code Grant](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/auth/public-authcode-get-token/) - [Authenticate with Implicit Code Grant](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/auth/implicit/) | Flow | When to use | Human present? | Client secret? | |------|-------------|---------------|----------------| | **JWT (Service Integration)** | Server/daemon jobs acting on behalf of a user after one-time consent has been granted. | No (after consent) | No (requires privateKey) | | **Confidential Authorization Code Grant** | Server-side applications that can securely store a secret. Supports refresh_token. | Yes (initial sign-in) | Yes | | **Public Authorization Code Grant (PKCE)** | SPA, Mobile, or Desktop apps that cannot securely store a secret. | Yes (initial sign-in) | No (uses PKCE flow) | ## Prerequisites Before obtaining the access token, ensure the following environment or collection variables are configured in Postman. | Variable | Example Value | Description | |----------|---------------|-------------| | ``{{​authServer}}`` | Demo: https://account-d.docusign.com
Production: https://account.docusign.com | Authentication base URL. | | ``{{​redirectUri}}`` | http://localhost:8080/ | A redirect URI (callback URI) where Docusign sends users after authentication. | | ``{{​origin}}`` | https://localhost:3000 | Origin URL for PKCE CORS requests. Must match an allowed origin in your Docusign [CORS configuration](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/configure-app/#cors-configuration). | | ``{{​scope}}`` | signature extended | Permissions requested (e.g., signature, impersonation for JWT). See: https://developers.docusign.com/platform/auth/reference/scopes/ | | ``{{​integrationKey}}`` | Example: f8db556a-xxxx-4f30-xxxx-648ea46axxxx | Integration key identifying your app (falls back to ``{{​IKey}}``). Create one: https://developers.docusign.com/platform/configure-app/#how-to-get-an-integration-key | | ``{{​clientSecret}}`` | Example: f2db256a-xxxx-4f30-xxxx-64a2346axxxx | Secret key for confidential integrations (falls back to ``{{​ISec}}``): https://developers.docusign.com/platform/configure-app/#secret-key | | ``{{​publicIntegrationKey}}`` | Example: f2db256a-xxxx-4f30-xxxx-64a2346axxxx | Public integration key used for PKCE flows. Falls back to ``{{​integrationKey}}``/``{{​IKey}}``. | | ``{{​userId}}`` | Example: f2db256a-xxxx-4f30-xxxx-64a2346axxxx | User GUID. Find it here: https://developers.docusign.com/platform/configure-app/#user-id | | ``{{​privateKey}}`` | -----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY----- ... -----END RSA PRIVATE KEY----- | RSA private key for JWT flow: https://developers.docusign.com/platform/configure-app/#rsa-key-pair | ## Steps to get token 1. JWT (Service Integration) 1. Open the **01 - JWT / Service Integration** folder. 2. Execute the GET request of **01-1 – Construct Consent URL**. 3. Open the returned **authorizeUrl** in a browser. 4. Sign in and grant consent (must include **impersonation**). 5. After consent is granted, you can close the browser. 6. Run **01-2 - JWT Token (exchange)** 2. Confidential Auth Code 1. Open the **02 – Authorization Code Grant (Confidential Client)** folder. 2. Execute the GET request of **02-1 – Construct Authorization URL**. 3. Open the returned **authorizeUrl** in a browser tab. 4. Sign in and grant consent. 5. Copy the **?code=** value from the URL and set it as ``{{​authCodeNoPkce}}``. 6. Run **02-2 – Exchange Authorization Code for Tokens**. 3. Public Auth Code (PKCE) 1. Open the **03 – Authorization Code Grant with PKCE (Public Client)** folder**.** 2. Execute the GET request of **03-1 – Construct Authorization URL with PKCE**. 3. Open the returned **authorizeUrl** in a browser tab. 4. Sign in and grant consent. 5. Copy the **?code=** value from the URL and set it as ``{{​authCodePkce}}``. 6. Ensure the Origin parameter added in Apps and Keys page is set in headers of  **03-2 – Exchange Authorization Code for Tokens (PKCE)** and CORS is activated with all the HTTP operations. For further information, refer [Set the CORS configuration of your app.](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/configure-app/#cors-configuration) 7. Run **03-2 – Exchange Authorization Code for Tokens (PKCE)**. 4. Execute 05 – User Info to verify your user details and base URI. ## Notes - If the access token has expired, execute 04 – Refresh Access Token using the refresh token. Default expiration is 8 hours (`expires_in` is in seconds). - Moving to Production: Switch ``{{​authServer}}`` to [https://account.docusign.com](https://account.docusign.com) and re-consent. The base URI can be accessed by executing 05 – User Info.

item.name: 01 - JWT / Service Integration

description.content:
This request demonstrates authentication using JSON Web Tokens (JWT) Grant for accessing APIs without user interaction.

1. Before making the first call, an administrator or the target user must grant consent to your **Integration Key** (Client ID) to allow specific scopes.To see the full list of all supported scopes, see [Authentication scopes](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/auth/reference/scopes/).
    
2. Your server constructs and digitally signs a **JWT** using your **private key** and the user's ID.
    
3. The signed JWT is exchanged with the authorization server for a short-lived access token. This access token is then used in subsequent API calls.
    

This workflow is ideal for background processes, scheduled tasks, and integrations that require impersonating a user without constant user interaction.

**Variables**

For context around what JWT Grant authentication is, when you should use it, and its advantages and disadvantages, see [JSON Web Token (JWT) Grant](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/auth/jwt/).

| Variable | Required | Notes | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| ``{{​integrationKey}}`` | ✅ | An integration key identifies your integration and links to its configuration values. [Create an integration key](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/configure-app/#how-to-get-an-integration-key). Falls back to ``{{​IKey}}``. | — |
| ``{{​userId}}`` | ✅ | Find your [User ID](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/configure-app/#user-id) in the Apps and Keys page. | — |
| ``{{​privateKey}}`` | ✅ | The **PEM formatted Private Key** used for RS256 signing the JWT. | — |
| ``{{​authServer}}`` | ✅ | Authentication base URL (use https://account.docusign.com for Production). | `https://account-d.docusign.com` |
| ``{{​scope}}`` | Optional | Permissions requested (e.g., signature, impersonation for JWT).To see the full list of all supported scopes, see [Authentication scopes](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/auth/reference/scopes/). | ``organization_read organization_write group_read user_read user_write account_read domain_read`` |
| ``{{​USE_CUSTOM_ASSERTION}}`` | Optional | `true` to use your own ``{{​jwt_assertion}}`` | `false` |

item.name: 01-1 - Construct Consent URL (JWT)

description.content:
## **Grant Consent for JWT (Impersonation)**

1. Execute this helper request.
    
2. Open the returned authorizeUrl in a browser.
    
3. Sign in and grant consent (must include `impersonation`).
    
4. After consent is granted, you can close the browser.
    
5. Run **01-2 - JWT Token (exchange)**.

exec: const authServer = u.getVar(pm, 'authServer', undefined, { required: true });
const integrationKey = u.getVar(pm, 'integrationKey', undefined, { required: true, aliases: ['IKey'] });
const redirect = u.encVar(pm, 'redirectUri', undefined, { required: true });
const prompt = 'Open the URL, sign in, grant consent (JWT / impersonation). When done, you can close the browser and run 01-2 - JWT Token (exchange).';

item.name: 01-2 - JWT Token (exchange)

description.content:
Exchanges the signed JWT (``{{​jwt_assertion}}``) for an access token.

**Two ways to supply the JWT assertion**
1) **Auto-mint (default):** Set ``{{​integrationKey}}`` (or ``{{​IKey}}``), ``{{​userId}}``, ``{{​authServer}}``, and ``{{​privateKey}}``. The script mints a JWT and sets ``{{​jwt_assertion}}`` for you.
2) **Custom assertion:** Set ``{{​USE_CUSTOM_ASSERTION}}`` to `true` **and** provide ``{{​jwt_assertion}}`` yourself (PEM not needed).

**Output:** ``{{​access_token}}``. (JWT does **not** return a refresh token.)

exec: // Require the caller to provide a ready-made JWT assertion
const custom = utils.getVar(pm, 'jwt_assertion', undefined, { required: true });
// Mint a new JWT assertion from key material
const integrationKey   = utils.getVar(pm, 'integrationKey',   undefined, { required: true, aliases: ['IKey'] });
const userId     = utils.getVar(pm, 'userId',     undefined, { required: true });
const authServer = utils.getVar(pm, 'authServer', undefined, { required: true });
const pem        = utils.getVar(pm, 'privateKey', undefined, { required: true });
const jwt = utils.mintJwt({ integrationKey, userId, authServer, privateKeyPem: pem, scope, expSeconds: 3600 });

item.name: 02 - Authorization Code Grant (Confidential Client)

description.content:
This folder implements the **Authorization Code Grant** flow. This process requires the user to be present initially to grant authorization through a browser-based login and consent screen.

Upon successful consent, the system receives an authorization code which is exchanged for both a short-lived access token and a long-lived refresh token.

The key benefit of this approach is the ability to use the refresh token to continually request new access tokens in the background, thereby maintaining long-lived user sessions and enabling your server to execute automated tasks without repeated user interaction.

**Variables**

Authorization Code Grant is a type of OAuth 2.0 flow used to obtain an access token. [Confidential Authorization Code Grant](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/auth/authcode/#confidential-authorization-code-grant), which should be used by apps that can secure a secret key.

| Variable | Required | Notes | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| ``{{​integrationKey}}`` | ✅ | An integration key identifies your integration and links to its configuration values. [Create an integration key](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/configure-app/#how-to-get-an-integration-key). Falls back to ``{{​IKey}}``. |  |
| ``{{​clientSecret}}`` | ✅ | The secret key identifies your integration app through a unique key. [Create a Secret Key](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/configure-app/#secret-key). Falls back to ``{{​ISec}}``. |  |
| ``{{​​redirectUri}}`` | ✅ | The URI is registered on your Docusign Integration Key (API Key). You can choose your application’s redirect uri. |  |
| ``{{​authServer}}`` | ✅ | Authentication base URL (use [https://account.docusign.com](https://account.docusign.com) for Production). | `https://account-d.docusign.com` |
| ``{{​​scope}}`` | Optional | Permissions requested (e.g., signature, impersonation for JWT).To see the full list of all supported scopes, see [Authentication scopes](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/auth/reference/scopes/). | ``organization_read organization_write group_read user_read user_write account_read domain_read`` |
| ``{{​authCodeNoPkce}}`` | ✅ (Only on exchange) | Paste the `?code` here (env or collection var) |  |

item.name: 02-1 - Construct Authorization URL

description.content:
## **Get the Authorization Code**

1. Execute the GET request of 02-1 - Construct Authorization URL.
    
2. Open the returned authorizeUrl and open in a browser tab.
    
3. Sign in, allow access and grant consent.
    
4. The browser will redirect to your redirectUri.
    
5. Copy the code value from the resulting URL query string.
    
6. Paste the copied code into a new or existing Environment or Collection Variable named ``{{​authCodeNoPkce}}``.

exec: const authServer = u.getVar(pm, 'authServer', undefined, { required: true });
const integrationKey   = u.getVar(pm, 'integrationKey',   undefined, { required: true, aliases: ['IKey'] });
const redirect   = u.encVar(pm, 'redirectUri', undefined, { required: true });

const prompt = 'Open the URL, sign in, grant consent, copy ?code= and paste it into an **environment or collection variable** named "authCodeNoPkce". Then run 02-2 - Exchange Authorization Code for Tokens.';

item.name: 02-2 - Exchange Authorization Code for Tokens

description.content:
## **Exchanging Code for Tokens**

This request exchanges the temporary Authorization Code for usable access and refresh tokens.

1. Verify that your environment or collection variables for ``{{​integrationKey}}`` (or ``{{​IKey}}``), ``{{​clientSecret}}`` (or ``{{​ISec}}``), and ``{{​authCodeNoPkce}}`` are all populated with the correct values.
    
2. Execute this request.
    
3. Check the response body: The response will contain the `access_token` and `refresh_token`.
    
4. The Pre-request or test script automatically extracts the access_token and refresh_token and saves them as environment variables. This lets you use the Bearer Token authentication instead of manual UI logins for later API calls.

exec: // Accept either variables OR literal body params for these inputs:
// Resolve IKey/ISec fallback for {{integrationKey}}/{{clientSecret}} body templates
var _ik = utils.getVar(pm, 'integrationKey', null, { aliases: ['IKey'] }); if (_ik) pm.variables.set('integrationKey', _ik);
var _cs = utils.getVar(pm, 'clientSecret', null, { aliases: ['ISec'] }); if (_cs) pm.variables.set('clientSecret', _cs);

item.name: 03 - Authorization Code Grant with PKCE (Public Client)

description.content:
This folder implements the **Public Authorization Code Grant (with PKCE)** flow, which is the required OAuth 2.0 standard for public applications like Single Page Applications (SPAs) and mobile apps.

This process requires the user to be present initially to grant authorization through a browser-based login and consent screen.

Upon successful consent, the system receives an authorization code which is exchanged for both a short-lived access token and a long-lived refresh token.

The key benefit of this approach is the ability to use the refresh token to continually request new access tokens in the background, thereby maintaining long-lived user sessions and enabling your server to execute automated tasks without repeated user interaction.

**Variables**

Public Authorization Code Grant (with PKCE) is a type of OAuth 2.0 flow used to obtain an access token. [Public Authorization Code Grant](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/auth/authcode/#public-authorization-code-grant), which does not require that your app secure a secret key.

| Variable | Required | Notes | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| ``{{​publicIntegrationKey}}`` | ✅ | An integration key identifies your integration and links to its configuration values. [Create an integration key](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/configure-app/#how-to-get-an-integration-key). If absent, it falls back to ``{{​integrationKey}}``. |  |
| ``{{​integrationKey}}`` | Fallback | An integration key identifies your integration and links to its configuration values. Falls back to ``{{​IKey}}``. [Create an integration key](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/configure-app/#how-to-get-an-integration-key). |  |
| ``{{​redirectUri}}`` | ✅ | The URI is registered on your Docusign Integration Key (API Key). You can choose your application’s redirect uri. |  |
| ``{{​authServer}}`` | ✅ | Authentication base URL (use [https://account.docusign.com](https://account.docusign.com) for Production). | `https://account-d.docusign.com` |
| ``{{​origin}}`` | ✅ (Only on exchange) | The Origin URL registered in your Docusign App CORS configuration. Must match an allowed origin in Apps and Keys. See [Set the CORS configuration of your app.](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/configure-app/#cors-configuration) |  |
| ``{{​scope}}`` | Optional | Permissions requested (e.g., signature, impersonation for JWT).To see the full list of all supported scopes, see [Authentication scopes](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/auth/reference/scopes/). | ``organization_read organization_write group_read user_read user_write account_read domain_read`` |

item.name: 03-1 - Construct Authorization URL with PKCE

description.content:
This request generates the authorization URL required to start the OAuth 2.0 flow.

## Get the Authorization Code

1. Execute the GET request of _03-1 - Construct Authorization URL with PKCE_.
    
2. Open the returned _authorizeUrl_ and open in a browser tab.
    
3. Sign in, allow access and grant consent.
    
4. The browser will redirect to your redirectUri.
    
5. Copy the _code_ value from the resulting URL query string.
    

Paste the copied _code_ into a new or existing Environment or Collection Variable named ``{{​authCodePkce}}``.

exec: const authServer = u.getVar(pm, 'authServer', undefined, { required: true });
const redirect   = u.encVar(pm, 'redirectUri', undefined, { required: true });
// prefer explicit publicIntegrationKey; otherwise require integrationKey
const integrationKey = pubId ? pubId : u.getVar(pm, 'integrationKey', undefined, { required: true, aliases: ['IKey'] });

const prompt = 'Open the URL, sign in, grant consent, copy ?code= and paste it into an **environment or collection variable** named "authCodePkce". Then run 03-2 - Exchange Authorization Code for Tokens (PKCE).';

item.name: 03-2 - Exchange Authorization Code for Tokens (PKCE)

description.content:
## Exchanging Code for Tokens

This request exchanges the temporary Authorization Code using PKCE for usable access and refresh tokens.

1. Verify that your environment or collection variables for ``{{​integrationKey}}`` (or ``{{​IKey}}``), ``{{​origin}}``, ``{{​authCodeWithPkce}}``, and the automatically generated ``{{​code_verifier}}`` are all populated with the correct values.
    
2. Ensure ``{{​origin}}`` matches the Origin registered on your Docusign Apps and Keys page and CORS is activated with all the HTTP operations. For further information, refer [Set the CORS configuration of your app.](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/configure-app/#cors-configuration)
    
3. Execute this request.
    
4. Check the response body: The response will contain the _access_token_ and _refresh_token_.
    
5. The Pre-request or test script should automatically extract and set the ``{{​access_token}}`` and ``{{​refresh_token}}`` variables for use in subsequent API calls.

exec: // Client ID can be body literal (client_id) OR a variable (publicIntegrationKey/integrationKey)
throw new Error('Missing client id. Set publicIntegrationKey/integrationKey/IKey or place a literal client_id in the body.');
if (!hasPublicVar) { var _ik = utils.getVar(pm, 'integrationKey', null, { aliases: ['IKey'] }); if (_ik) pm.variables.set('publicIntegrationKey', _ik); }

item.name: 04 - Refresh Access Token

description.content:
This request uses the long-lived refresh token to obtain a new access token, ensuring continuous API access without user re-authentication.

1. Verify that the ``{{​refresh_token}}`` variable is populated from the previous "Exchange Code for Tokens" request.
    
2. Execute this request.
    
3. The response will contain a brand new _access_token_.
    
4. The test script should automatically extract and overwrite the old value of the ``{{​access_token}}`` variable with the new token.

exec: // Resolve IKey/ISec fallback for body/auth templates
var _ik = utils.getVar(pm, 'integrationKey', null, { aliases: ['IKey'] }); if (_ik) pm.variables.set('integrationKey', _ik);
var _cs = utils.getVar(pm, 'clientSecret', null, { aliases: ['ISec'] }); if (_cs) pm.variables.set('clientSecret', _cs);

item.name: 05 - User Info

description.content:
The **User Info** request serves as the validation step following any successful OAuth 2.0 flow. It confirms the access token's validity and retrieves the fundamental identity details of your Docusign account.

- **Identity Validation:** The primary response fields, such as `"sub"` (the user ID), `"name"`, and `"email"`, confirms **who** the application is acting on behalf of.
    
- **API Configuration:.** The default account contains the `account_id` (`1b63b53e-xxxx-4728-xxxx-e5aef8f305d1`) and the correct `base_uri`. Your application must extract these values to construct the correct endpoints for making calls like sending envelopes or managing templates.
    

This request validates the entire authentication process and supplies all the required user and account context to interact with the Docusign API.

exec: pm.test('✅ Parsed userinfo (optional vars stored if present)', () => pm.expect(true).to.equal(true));
* utils: Shared helpers for the Auth folder (folder-level pre-request).
* These run before each request in this folder. Pass `pm` into helpers.
/** isString(v): true if v is a non-empty string */
const isString = v => typeof v === 'string' && v.trim() !== '';
/** isPlaceholder(v): true if value looks like a {{variable}} */
const isPlaceholder = v => isString(v) && v.startsWith('{{') && v.endsWith('}}');
/** asciiSafe(s): strip non-ASCII for header-safe echo endpoints */
return `Missing ${name}. Set it in your active Environment or in Collection variables.`;
* Resolve var from env → collection → globals, then try each alias.
function getVar(pm_, name, fallback, { required = false, aliases = [] } = {}){
if (required) { throw new Error(_missingMsg(aliases.length ? name + ' (or ' + aliases.join('/') + ')' : name)); }
/** encVar(pm, name, fallback, opt): getVar + encodeURIComponent (unless placeholder) */
const v = getVar(pm_, name, fallback, opt);
/** getScope(pm): returns scope string (defaults to 'organization_read organization_write group_read user_read user_write account_read domain_read') */
return getVar(pm_, 'scope', 'organization_read organization_write group_read user_read user_write account_read domain_read');
/** deriveBaseHost(url): strips scheme or uses URL.host */
* Safely add/remove a urlencoded body param by key using body.update().
const next = enabled ? filtered.concat({ key, value: '', type: 'text' }) : filtered;
// ---- presence checks in urlencoded vs vars --------------------------------
function requireVarOrUrlencoded(pm_, varName, bodyKey, displayName, { aliases = [] } = {}){
const hasVar = names.some(n => isString(pm_.environment.get(n)) || isString(pm_.collectionVariables.get(n)) || isString(pm_.globals.get(n)));
* ensurePkce(pm): returns { codeVerifier, codeChallenge }, generating if missing.
* mintJwt({ integrationKey, userId, authServer, privateKeyPem, scope, expSeconds=3600 })
* Signs an RS256 JWT for Docusign OAuth (aud = authServer host).
function mintJwt({ integrationKey, userId, authServer, privateKeyPem, scope, expSeconds = 3600 }){

const body = { iss: integrationKey, sub: userId, aud, iat: now, exp: now + expSeconds, scope: scope || 'organization_read organization_write group_read user_read user_write account_read domain_read' };
// ---- Token storage (env → collection → runtime; snake_case only) -------
try { pm_.environment.set(key, val); return true; } catch(_) {}
try { pm_.collectionVariables.set(key, val); return true; } catch(_) {}
try { pm_.variables.set(key, val); return true; } catch(_) {}
* storeTokensFromResponse(pm, r): saves tokens to env/collection/runtime in snake_case.
* Sets: access_token, refresh_token, token_type, expires_in, access_token_expires_at, received_at.
getVar: (pm_, n, f, o)=>getVar(pm_, n, f, o), encVar: (pm_, n, f, o)=>encVar(pm_, n, f, o), getScope: (pm_)=>getScope(pm_),
setUrlencodedEnabled: (pm_, k, e)=>setUrlencodedEnabled(pm_, k, e), ensurePkce: (pm_)=>ensurePkce(pm_),
hasUrlencoded: (pm_, k)=>hasUrlencoded(pm_, k), requireVarOrUrlencoded: (pm_, vn, bk, dn, o)=>requireVarOrUrlencoded(pm_, vn, bk, dn, o),

item.name: IdentityProviders

description.content: Methods to get a list of identity providers.

request.name: Returns the list of identity providers for an organization.

request.name: Returns the list of reserved domains for the organization.

description.content: Methods to manage users in an account.

request.name: Updates a user's email address.

request.name: Returns information about the users in an organization.

description.content: Index of first item to include in the response. The default value is 0.
Page size of the response. The default value is 20.
Index of the last item to include in the response. Ignored if `take` parameter is specified.
Email address of the desired user. At least one of `email`, `account_id` or `organization_reserved_domain_id` must be specified.
Selects users by pattern matching on the user's email address

The user's membership status. One of:

- `activation_required`
- `activation_sent`
- `active`
- `closed`
- `disabled`
Select users that are members of the specified account. At least one of `email`, `account_id` or `organization_reserved_domain_id` must be specified.
Select users that are in the specified domain. At least one of `email`, `account_id` or `organization_reserved_domain_id` must be specified.
Select users whose data have been modified since the date specified. `account_id` or `organization_reserved_domain_id` must be specified.
Select users with groups the users belong to; The organization must have entitlement `AllowMultiApplication` enabled.
When true: additional details about the user's license will be included in the response; account_id must be specified and the account must have an IAM plan with licenses.
Index of first item to include in the response. The default value is 0.
Page size of the response. The default value is 20.
Index of the last item to include in the response. Ignored if `take` parameter is specified.
Email address of the desired user. At least one of `email`, `account_id` or `organization_reserved_domain_id` must be specified.
Selects users by pattern matching on the user's email address

The user's membership status. One of:

- `activation_required`
- `activation_sent`
- `active`
- `closed`
- `disabled`
Select users that are members of the specified account. At least one of `email`, `account_id` or `organization_reserved_domain_id` must be specified.
Select users that are in the specified domain. At least one of `email`, `account_id` or `organization_reserved_domain_id` must be specified.
Select users whose data have been modified since the date specified. `account_id` or `organization_reserved_domain_id` must be specified.
Select users with groups the users belong to; The organization must have entitlement `AllowMultiApplication` enabled.

description.content: Methods to manage eSignature users in an account.

item.name: Returns the list of permission profiles in an account.

request.name: Returns the list of permission profiles in an account.

item.name: Returns the list of groups in an account.

request.name: Returns the list of groups in an account.

description.content: Index of first item to include in the response. The default value is 0.
Page size of the response. The default value is 20.
Index of the last item to include in the response. Ignored if `take` parameter is specified.
Index of first item to include in the response. The default value is 0.

Methods to import users.

To ensure your CSV is properly formatted, use the
[Sample Bulk Add CSV file](https://admin.docusign.com/static-resources/organization-user-import.csv)
 as a template.

You can add up to 2,000 users to an account and include up to 50 accounts per import. The maximum number of users per import is 8,000.

item.name: Organization
Returns a list of organizations that the authenticated user belongs to.

request.name: Returns a list of organizations that the authenticated user belongs to.

description.content:
Returns a list of organizations that the authenticated user belongs to.

Note that the Docusign Admin API only returns JSON, not XML.

[Required authentication scopes](/docs/admin-api/admin101/auth/): `organization_read`.

This method requires user authentication. It will fail with an [application access token](/docs/admin-api/admin101/application-auth/).

Specifies how to select the organizations. Valid values:

- `org_admin`: Returns organizations for which the authenticated user is an admin.
- `account_membership`: Returns organizations that contain an account of which the authenticated user is a member

Default value: `org_admin`

item.name: DataDeletion

description.content:
Methods to delete user data. After a user is closed, an account or organization administrator can use these methods to delete the user's account data.

### Next steps
* Learn [how to delete user data from an account as an account administrator](/docs/admin-api/how-to/account-admin-delete-user-data/).
* Learn [how to delete user data from multiple accounts as an organization administrator](/docs/admin-api/how-to/org-admin-delete-user-data/).

item.name: AccountCloning
Get asset group accounts for an organization.

request.name: Get asset group accounts for an organization.

description.content:
Returns a list of asset group accounts for an organization.

An asset group is a set of modules, products, plans, and charges purchased for your organization. An asset group account is an account that has been linked to an asset group.

To call this endpoint:
* You must be an administrator for the specified organization.

[Required authentication scopes](/docs/admin-api/admin101/auth/): `asset_group_account_read`.

### Next steps
* See [how to use this endpoint in your chosen programming language](/docs/admin-api/how-to/clone-account/).
When **true,** only compliant accounts are returned and account responses do not include the `compliant` field. The default value is **false.**
When **true,** only compliant accounts are returned and account responses do not include the `compliant` field. The default value is **false.**

item.name: Clone an existing Docusign account.

request.name: Clone an existing Docusign account.

description.content:
Clones an existing Docusign account. Cloning an account copies the plan items and eSignature settings. Users, templates, and permission profiles are _not_ copied into the target account. The new account will linked to the same organization.

In the request body, specify the source account you are cloning by its ID. You can get the IDs for your organization's asset group accounts using the [getAssetGroupAccounts](/docs/admin-api/reference/accountprovisioning/accountcloning/getassetgroupaccounts/) endpoint. 

You also need to specify information about the new target account, including the name, location, and administrator. To set the location, provide either the `countryCode` or `region` properties. (If both are specified, the `region` value will be used.) When testing in the developer environment, the region will always be `NA`.

The request body looks like this:
```
{
    "sourceAccount": {
        "id": "624e3e00-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-43918c520dab"
    },
    "targetAccount": {
        "name": "My Cloned Account",
        "admin": {
            "firstName": "Francis",
            "lastName": "Beagle",
            "email": "francis@example.com"
        },
        "region": "NA"
    }
}
```

To call this endpoint:
* You must be an administrator for the specified organization. The administrator for the target account must also be an organization admin.
* The source account must be on the same plan as your contract.
* The source account must be an asset group account. An asset group is a set of modules, products, plans, and charges purchased for your organization. An asset group account is an account that has been linked to an asset group.
* Your organization must be set up for account cloning. For more information, email self-serve-provisioning-enablement@docusign.com.

[Required scopes](/docs/admin-api/admin101/auth/): `asset_group_account_clone_write`.

### Next steps
* See [how to use this endpoint to clone an account](/docs/admin-api/how-to/clone-account/).

Retrieves all the cloned accounts for an organization.

* You must be an administrator for the specified organization.

[Required scopes](/docs/admin-api/admin101/auth/): `asset_group_account_clone_read`.
Use this parameter to retrieve only account clones that were created on or after a specified date.
When **true,** include additional details for the asset group account clones. The default value is **false.**
Use this parameter to retrieve only account clones that were created on or after a specified date.
When **true,** include additional details for the asset group account clones. The default value is **false.**

Required scopes: `connect_org_config_read_api`
Sorts configurations by the specified definition field in ascending order. Defaults to `name`, if not set.
Returns only configurations with the specified `siteId`.
Returns only configurations that apply to the specified `accountId`.
Returns only configurations that do (true) or do not (false) allow [envelope publishing](/docs/esign-rest-api/reference/envelopes/envelopepublish/).
Returns only configurations that have the `connectId` or `name` provided in the query parameter.
(Required) The GUID ID of the organization owning the Organization-Level Connect configurations.

item.name: Creates a new Connect HMAC secret for an organization.

Creates a new [HMAC](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/webhooks/connect/hmac/) secret for an organization. The response for this call will contain the full unredacted secret. Record this secret to a secure location, as you will not be able to get this information again later.

Required scopes: `connect_org_hmac_write_api`
(Required) The GUID ID of the organization within which the HMAC secret will be created.
